supply_chain.dependencies: Some raw materials are purchased from a limited set of suppliers, including international sources. Some items purchased for manufacture are from limited or single sources of supply due to technical capability, price, and other factors. Sole source supply is more common among businesses heavily involved in research and development. China is a key supplier for certain materials including gallium and germanium used in semiconductor manufacturing.
supply_chain.risks: Disruption of supply sources or supplier-imposed rationing of scarce components could cause delays or reductions in shipments. International sources pose additional risks including political instability, export restrictions, and currency fluctuations. Component and raw material shortages have impacted ability to manufacture and ship products. Supply chain constraints resulted in sustained increases in prices for components and raw materials. China has unveiled restrictions on exports of gallium and germanium which has impacted production and pricing of digital imaging products. Counterfeit parts risk could be exacerbated by supply chain shortages or parts becoming obsolete.

regulatory_risks: Export control regulations including ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations)
Export Administration Regulations (EAR) compliance
U.S. Department of State DDTC (Office of Defense Trade Controls Compliance) regulations
Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) export restrictions
Economic sanctions and export controls related to China
Sanctions related to Russia
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance
UK Bribery Act compliance
EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
Personal Information Protection Law in China
Non-U.S. data protection laws
ESG and CSR regulations in non-U.S. jurisdictions
Federal Acquisition Regulation cybersecurity requirements
Department of Defense cybersecurity regulations
Department of Homeland Security cybersecurity requirements
Department of Energy cybersecurity regulations
FAA (Federal Aviation Administration) regulations for commercial aviation products
U.S. Environmental Protection Agency regulations
Environmental regulations (REACH, RoHS Directives in Europe)
Registration, Evaluation, Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals Directives
Restriction of hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment Directives
Price-Anderson Nuclear Industries Indemnity Act
False Claims Act exposure
Climate change regulations and carbon tax proposals
Greenhouse gas emission reduction requirements
Federal deficit and debt reduction measures affecting defense spending
Trade sanctions and tariffs (including proposed tariffs by new Presidential administration)
Foreign direct investment laws and merger control regulations
In-country manufacturing requirements (China, India, Saudi Arabia)
Noncompetition and nonsolicitation agreement enforcement limitations

sanction_risks: Comprehensive sanctions on Russia imposed by multiple countries
U.S. export restrictions targeting Chinese companies and subsidiaries
Sectoral sanctions limiting Chinese development of semiconductor and supercomputer technology
Restrictions on U.S.-origin items and non-U.S. items manufactured from U.S.-origin equipment to China
China retaliatory export restrictions on gallium and germanium
Potential escalation of U.S.-China trade war
Increased tariffs and trade restrictions proposed by new U.S. Presidential administration
Restricted party lists impacting established customers and suppliers
Risk of being pressured by foreign governments not to purchase U.S.-origin goods
